topic: The Role of Markets and Money
subject: economics

items.q: What are the functions of money?
items.correct: Medium of exchange, store of value, unit of account, and standard of deferred payment — money must perform these to function as money.
items.misconception: Money is valuable because it is made of valuable materials.
items.random_wrong: Money is only valuable if backed by gold.
items.why: Modern fiat money has no intrinsic value — its value comes from social acceptance and government issuance. Students think paper money 'must' be backed by something; it isn't, and hasn't been since 1971.

items.q: What is the predicted effect of a binding price ceiling like rent control?
items.correct: Shortage in the market — quantity demanded exceeds quantity supplied at the controlled price, leading to queues, black markets and reduced quality.
items.misconception: Price ceilings make goods cheaper for everyone with no downsides.
items.random_wrong: Price ceilings only affect the rich.
items.why: Students see price control as obviously beneficial. Standard analysis predicts shortages and unintended consequences — the trade-off is central to evaluation questions on intervention.

items.q: How does quantitative easing actually work?
items.correct: The central bank creates reserves to buy financial assets (mostly government bonds) from banks and pension funds, raising asset prices and lowering yields — stimulating spending and lending indirectly. It is not literally printing physical cash for the public.
items.misconception: QE is the central bank printing money and handing it to citizens.
items.random_wrong: QE is when banks charge negative interest on deposits.
items.why: QE works through asset markets and bank reserves, not direct cash transfers. Students picture printing presses and helicopter drops, missing the indirect channel via bond yields, exchange rates and wealth effects.

items.q: A market with one seller is a:
items.why: Correct answer: Monopoly. “Perfect competition” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: The price mechanism performs three functions; which is NOT one?
items.why: Correct answer: Producing. “Signalling” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: What is the minimum number of sellers in a monopoly?
items.why: Exact answer: 1 seller. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
